items.question: What is CYO?
items.answer: CYO stands for Catholic Youth Organization, founded in Chicago in 1930. It expanded internationally and began in Pleasanton in 1971, offering basketball, track & field, and cross country programs.

items.question: What is CCOP?
items.answer: Our parish is CCOP — The Catholic Community of Pleasanton, which has two campus locations: St. Augustine and St. Elizabeth Ann Seton.

items.question: What does the CCOP CYO program involve?
items.answer: A parish ministry for grades 3–8 with community volunteers as coaches and leaders. The organization has co-sponsorship status with the City of Pleasanton and participates in the Tri-Valley CYO League.

items.question: Who is eligible to participate?
items.answer: Registered CCOP parishioners, residents of Pleasanton or Sunol, and residents of other cities enrolled in the CCOP Faith Formation Program.

items.question: What grade levels can participate?
items.answer: Our league is open to boys and girls in 3rd to 8th grade. We divide participants into age groups based on their current grade level.

items.question: Can non-Catholics play?
items.answer: Absolutely! Our CYO basketball league is open to participants of all faiths and beliefs. We embrace diversity and welcome all players.

items.question: What is the registration fee?
items.answer: Regular registration costs $375. Early bird registration (through July 15) is $350.

items.question: Are financial hardship waivers available?
items.answer: We offer a limited number of scholarships each year. Send a request to our Athletic Director(s) with additional details.

items.question: What is the refund policy?
items.answer: If a player cancels before evaluations begin, a 100% refund will be given. If the cancellation occurs during or after evaluations, a refund will not be honored.

items.question: How are teams formed? Can my child play with friends?
items.answer: We make every effort to honor requests for players to be placed on the same team as friends or classmates, while also ensuring teams remain balanced.

items.question: When does the basketball season run?
items.answer: Our basketball season generally starts in October and runs through January/February. The exact dates will be provided closer to the season start.

items.question: Do games stop for holidays?
items.answer: During Christmas and New Year's, league games are not scheduled. There are a few tournament opportunities that take place during the holiday breaks.

items.question: Where are practices and games held?
items.answer: Practices are held in Pleasanton at St. Elizabeth's Gym or one of the Pleasanton middle school gyms. Games occur once a week on Sunday afternoons throughout the Tri-Valley area.

items.question: How frequently do practices and games occur?
items.answer: Teams have two practices per week based on the coach's schedule and gym availability. Games are typically scheduled once a week on Sunday afternoons.

items.question: Is the TeamSnap app required?
items.answer: It is not mandatory, but makes coordination with the coaches a lot easier.

items.question: What equipment do children need?
items.answer: Players are required to have appropriate basketball shoes, athletic attire, and a water bottle. The league will provide team jerseys and game balls.

items.question: Can my child practice on specific days?
items.answer: We do not assign registered players to specific practice days or times. After evaluations, your child will be placed on a team led by a volunteer coach who will set the practice schedule.

items.question: Can parents volunteer as coaches?
items.answer: Absolutely! We encourage parent and guardian involvement as coaches, assistant coaches, and team helpers.

items.question: How do I become a coach?
items.answer: We welcome individuals with a passion for basketball and mentoring young athletes. To become a coach, please visit our website and complete the coaching registration form.

items.question: What coaching qualifications are needed?
items.answer: While prior coaching experience is preferred, it is not mandatory. We value individuals who are committed to positive player development and creating supportive environments.

items.question: Can I coach my child's team?
items.answer: Yes, you can coach your child's team if desired. We understand the importance of family involvement but encourage fairness and equal opportunities for all players.

items.question: How should coaches communicate with parents?
items.answer: Clear and consistent communication is vital. We recommend holding a pre-season meeting to discuss team expectations, practice and game schedules, and using TeamSnap for ongoing communication.

items.question: Who can become a referee?
items.answer: 9th through 12th grade high school students. CCOP alumni and Pleasanton residents are given priority.

items.question: Will referees receive training?
items.answer: Yes, training will be provided and is mandatory for all selected referees.

items.question: When is the referee season?
items.answer: Referee season starts as early as October and typically runs through mid-February.

items.question: What are referee working hours?
items.answer: Games are on Sundays and typically run from noon to 6pm. Referees are required to arrive 15 minutes prior to their first scheduled game.

items.question: Do returning referees need to reapply?
items.answer: Yes, all interested individuals must apply for the upcoming season.

items.question: How are referees paid?
items.answer: Referees receive a cash stipend for each game they work. Please contact the Referee Coordinator for details.
